2606.24986.title: When Multi-Sensor Fusion Fails to Generalize: Cattle Posture Classification Under Animal-Level and Temporal Distribution Shift
2606.24986.paper_summary: Automated cattle posture-classification systems frequently report near-perfect accuracy, yet their robustness under realistic deployment conditions remains largely unknown. In particular, it is unclear whether multimodal sensor fusion improves generalisation or leads models to rely on context-specific signals that fail under distribution shift. Here, we evaluate the robustness of automated posture classification (lying versus standing) using collar accelerometers, rumen-bolus sensors, and environmental measurements collected from a pasture-based beef cattle herd across two consecutive years (2024-2025). XGBoost served as the primary model, with Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and Long Short-Term Memory networks evaluated as comparative baselines. Model robustness was assessed under progressively more stringent evaluation protocols, ranging from conventional random train-test splits to leave-one-animal-out validation and cross-year evaluation on an independent cohort of previously unseen animals recorded one year later. While multimodal models achieved strong within-year performance (macro-F1 0.94), the performance declined substantially under cross-year evaluation (macro-F1 0.49). Explainability analysis revealed persistent reliance on rumen-bolus activity and environmental variables even when predictive performance deteriorated. Distribution-shift diagnostics further confirmed substantial differences in feature distributions between recording years. Our findings demonstrate that commonly used evaluation protocols can substantially overestimate real-world performance and that multimodal sensor fusion may reduce, rather than improve, robustness under temporal distribution shift. More broadly, the results highlight that benchmark accuracy alone is insufficient to assess deployment readiness and underscore the need for robustness-centred evaluation in livestock-monitoring research.
2606.24986.paper_summary_zh: 自動化的牛隻姿勢分類系統經常報告接近完美的準確率，但它們在現實部署條件下的穩健性仍然大部分未知。特別是，目前尚不清楚多模態傳感器融合是否改善了泛化能力，或導致模型依賴於在分佈轉移下失效的特定上下文信號。在這裡，我們使用項圈加速度計、瘤胃球傳感器和從一個基於牧場的肉牛群中收集的環境測量，評估自動化姿勢分類（躺著與站立）的穩健性，這些數據收集跨越兩個連續年份（2024-2025）。XGBoost作為主要模型，邏輯回歸、隨機森林和長短期記憶網絡則被評估為比較基準。模型的穩健性在逐步更嚴格的評估協議下進行評估，這些協議從傳統的隨機訓練-測試劃分到留一隻動物驗證，以及在獨立的先前未見動物群體上進行跨年評估。雖然多模態模型在年度內表現強勁（宏F1 0.94），但在跨年評估中表現顯著下降（宏F1 0.49）。可解釋性分析顯示，即使在預測性能惡化的情況下，仍然持續依賴於瘤胃球活動和環境變數。分佈轉移診斷進一步確認了錄製年份之間特徵分佈的顯著差異。我們的研究結果表明，常用的評估協議可能會大幅高估現實世界的性能，而多模態傳感器融合可能在時間分佈轉移下減少而非改善穩健性。更廣泛地說，這些結果強調僅依賴基準準確率不足以評估部署準備性，並強調在牲畜監測研究中需要以穩健性為中心的評估。
2606.24986.author: Leutrim Uka et.al.
2606.24986.authors: Leutrim Uka, Severino Pinto, Gundula Hoffmann, Marina M. -C. Höhne
